title: Trophaeum emarginatum
taxonomy: Trophaeum emarginatum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Trophaeum and is a part of the family of plants known as dogbane or oleander. The classification of Trophaeum emarginatum is still under scientific study and research, but it is known to be a type of evergreen shrub native to tropical regions. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Trophaeum emarginatum is closely related to other flowering plants such as Mandevilla and Echites. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and characteristics of Trophaeum emarginatum.

The plant is traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
